title: Session-spawn conversation distillation into the distillation pipeline (folded WP-1-Session-Spawn-Conversation-Distillation)
goal: Feed spawn-tree conversation histories into the cluster C distillation pipeline so child-session summaries become training data for parent-role specialization. Folded from WP-1-Session-Spawn-Conversation-Distillation. A spawn tree represents a structured decomposition of complex work; parent-child conversation trails are high-quality teacher-student data. MUST honor AC-DISTILL-OPT-IN (default OFF; per-session opt-in only).
expected_diff_shape: New module src/backend/handshake_core/src/distillation/spawn_tree_harvester.rs (SpawnTreeHarvester + SpawnConversation + DistillationCandidate types). Wires into cluster C distillation pipeline by trait. Tests src/backend/handshake_core/tests/spawn_tree_harvester_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-145 (CapsuleRecorder for storing distillation candidates), MT-158 (outcome feedback used to gate which spawn trees are candidate-worthy). Cluster C distillation pipeline trait surface consumed externally.
implementation_notes: Per WP-1-Session-Spawn-Conversation-Distillation preserved_intent: 'A spawn tree represents a structured decomposition of complex work. The parent-child conversation trail is high-quality training signal for model specialization'. Per AC-DISTILL-OPT-IN: 'Distillation is default OFF for every governed session; opt-in is per-session (never persistent default) via operator setting DISTILL_CORPUS=true at session close (post-hoc); scope of opt-in is a single session's events + artifacts and does not extend transitively'. Per AC-DISTILL-CONTENT-REVIEW: 'Before any distillation candidate enters the Skill Bank it MUST pass: PII scan + license tagging + dedup'. Surface: `pub struct SpawnTreeHarvester<'a> { event_ledger: &'a dyn EventLedgerReader, content_reviewer: &'a dyn ContentReviewer, action_catalog: &'a dyn KernelActionSubmitter }` with `pub fn harvest(session_id: Uuid, opt_in_confirmed: bool) -> Result<Vec<DistillationCandidate>, HarvestError>`. Flow: (1) refuse if opt_in_confirmed is false (AC-DISTILL-OPT-IN); (2) walk spawn tree from session_id; (3) extract parent-child conversation pairs as SpawnConversation { parent_message, child_response, child_outcome (from MT-158) }; (4) filter to pairs where child_outcome is Pass (otherwise teacher-student signal is poisoned); (5) submit each to content_reviewer (PII + license + dedup); (6) emit FR-EVT-DISTILL-CANDIDATE-CREATED for each surviving candidate; (7) submit DistillationCandidate via KernelActionCatalogV1 for review-gated Skill Bank entry. DistillationCandidate: `{ candidate_id: Uuid v7, session_id, parent_message_id, child_message_id, parent_role, child_role, content_hash, license_provenance, pii_scan_result }`. Tests: harvest with opt_in_confirmed=false returns typed error; harvest with opted-in session walks spawn tree; only Pass-outcome pairs are candidates; content_reviewer is invoked for every candidate; harvest emits FR-EVT-DISTILL-CANDIDATE-CREATED for surviving candidates; harvest does NOT auto-promote (review-gated).
proof_commands: $env:CARGO_TARGET_DIR='D:/Projects/LLM projects/Handshake/Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-cargo-target'; cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features test-utils --test fems_v1_integration_tests spawn_tree_harvester -- --nocapture
risk_if_missed: Without spawn-tree distillation the parent-child conversation signal is wasted, and the only training data is post-hoc EventLedger replay without structural decomposition.
validator_focus: Opt-in is enforced before any harvest. Content review is invoked for every candidate. Only Pass-outcome pairs are candidates (avoid teaching from failures).

operator_clarification_20260520.summary: This is a SECONDARY distillation flow (intra-session parent-child spawn-tree pairs as teacher-student data), parallel to the PRIMARY distillation flow built by MT-119/120/121/122/123/124. Under operator's 2026-05-20 clarification, the primary flow defaults to CLOUD-TEACHER -> LOCAL-STUDENT (frontier vendor model teaching a local student). This MT-166 flow is the local-larger-teacher modality the operator described as 'supported but not default': parent role (acting as larger-context teacher) -> specialized child role (student). Both flows coexist; this one does not replace cloud-teacher distillation.
operator_clarification_20260520.prior_assumption_corrected: There was no prior incorrect assumption attached to this MT specifically; the clarification is forward-context so the implementer understands which distillation modality this MT belongs to and how it relates to the cloud-teacher-primary flow.
implications: This flow is local-to-local by nature (parent and child are both Handshake-internal sessions); no cloud teacher transport needed for THIS flow. Cloud-teacher distillation is the responsibility of MT-119/120/122 corpus extraction + PEFT pipeline, not this MT.
DISTILL_CORPUS opt-in semantics (MT-121) apply identically: spawn-tree pairs only enter the corpus when the session is flagged DISTILL_CORPUS=true at close. Default-deny per AC-DISTILL-OPT-IN.

Output of this MT feeds the SAME PEFT pipeline (MT-122) as cloud-teacher corpus; the pipeline must accept both teacher-source modalities transparently. Teacher ModelId in the training run is the parent-role ModelId for this flow, the cloud-vendor ModelId for the cloud-teacher flow.

License/provenance tagging (MT-120 pipeline reused) is simpler for this flow (no third-party vendor ToS to honor), but the spawn-tree provenance MUST still be captured: which parent role + which child role + which task spawned the pair. Operator-controlled internal data, but provenance is non-optional.

Pass-outcome filtering (only successful parent-child pairs become candidates) is unique to this flow and does NOT apply to cloud-teacher distillation — keep the filter local to this MT, not in the shared MT-120 pipeline.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Opt-in is enforced before any harvest; harvest with opt_in_confirmed=false returns typed error.
Only Pass-outcome parent-child pairs are candidates; failure pairs cannot leak as teacher-student data.
Content reviewer is invoked for every candidate; no candidate bypasses PII + license + dedup.

implementation_evidence.summary: MT-166 completed session-spawn conversation distillation hardening. Existing harvester opt-in/pass filtering/content review remains, and candidate submission now carries an explicit FR-EVT-DISTILL-CANDIDATE-CREATED event projection, action id, review-gate state, no-auto-promote flag, and non-authoritative training refs.

sources_checked: LangSmith trace-to-dataset and dataset evaluation/versioning docs: https://docs.langchain.com/langsmith/manage-datasets
OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions for observable AI traces: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/
Hugging Face dataset cards / dataset governance reference: https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/datasets-cards
Hugging Face datasets processing and filtering docs: https://huggingface.co/docs/datasets/process
relevant_patterns_found: Training/evaluation examples should preserve trace/dataset provenance and stable identifiers instead of relying on raw conversational transcript authority.
Dataset candidates need explicit curation gates for PII, license, dedup, and review status before promotion.
Trace-derived examples should carry observable metadata/event references so downstream agents can audit how a training item was created.
reuse_opportunities: Reused existing EventLedgerReader, ContentReviewer, SpawnPair, and pass-outcome filtering surface in spawn_tree_harvester.rs.
Reused MT-158 CapsuleOutcome::Pass as the poisoned-signal filter.
Reused existing FEMS V1 integration smoke and added the missing dedicated spawn_tree_harvester_tests integration binary.
rejected_options: Rejected auto-promoting candidates to Skill Bank because MT-166 requires review-gated entry.
Rejected making raw conversation text authoritative; submission now emits typed refs instead.

Rejected wiring full KernelActionSubmitter inside the harvester because it would pull write-box construction into a pure extraction component; kept a submitter trait and recorded adapter need as residual risk.
research_basis.selected_approach: Keep harvester pure and contract-shaped while extending DistillationCandidateSubmitter to accept DistillationCandidateSubmission containing action id, candidate, candidate-created event metadata, review gate, no-auto-promote flag, and training refs.
risks: A concrete KernelActionCatalogV1 adapter is not implemented in this microtask; submitter trait remains the integration boundary.
Event emission is represented in the submission envelope; a downstream adapter must persist/forward it to Flight Recorder.
ContentReviewer returns a simplified pass/quarantine/reject verdict and the candidate pii_scan_result is clean only for passing verdicts.
mitigations: Submission envelope includes action_id and event metadata so the adapter has deterministic material to persist.
Tests assert review_gate PendingSkillBankReview and auto_promote false.
Tests assert opt-in false fails before ledger read and reviewer/submission calls.
validation_plan: Run existing FEMS V1 spawn_tree_harvester smoke tests.
Run rustfmt --check, git diff --check, and forbidden SQLite/model-runtime scans on touched files.

red.note: Initial red run failed because CandidateReviewGate and DistillationCandidateSubmission did not exist; current submitter only accepted a bare candidate.

green.note: 3 passed: opt-in false short-circuit, pass/review/filter/event/review-gate submission, and non-authoritative training refs.

out_of_owned_files_pending_operator_revi: src/backend/handshake_core/tests/fems_v1_integration_tests.rs (updated existing MT-166 smoke submitter to new submission envelope trait)
high_roi_additions: Dedicated spawn_tree_harvester_tests binary matching the MT-166 contract.
Explicit candidate-created event projection for every surviving candidate.
Review-gate/no-auto-promote fields so downstream Skill Bank promotion cannot be implied by harvest.
Training-example/teacher/student refs with conversation_text_authority=false to avoid raw transcript authority drift.

residual_risks: No Integration Validator has reviewed MT-166; READY_FOR_VALIDATION is implementer handoff only.
A concrete KernelActionCatalogV1/Flight Recorder adapter for DistillationCandidateSubmission remains a follow-up integration concern if the validator requires persistence beyond the trait boundary.
The harvester assumes EventLedgerReader already scopes returned SpawnPair rows to the requested session/spawn tree.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: SpawnTreeHarvester enforces AC-DISTILL-OPT-IN via opt_in_confirmed=false short-circuit returning HarvestError::OptInRequired before any ledger/reviewer/submitter calls. Only Pass-outcome pairs proceed to ContentReviewer; Quarantine + Reject verdicts continue past (correctly excluded). DistillationCandidateSubmission carries review_gate=PendingSkillBankReview and auto_promote=false; training/teacher/student refs are non-authoritative. 3 + 2 + 4 tests reported pass. CONCERN: opt-in non-transitivity is asserted by the harvester only at the top-level session_id; the spawn-tree transitivity (parent session A spawns child session B; only A is opted in; do we walk B's events?) is delegated to the EventLedgerReader implementation, NOT enforced at the harvester layer. The MT-169 negative-path test for non-transitivity uses two PEER sessions in the same ledger, not a true parent-child spawn relationship.

CONCERN: opt-in non-transitivity is asserted by the harvester only at the top-level session_id; the spawn-tree transitivity (parent session A spawns child session B; only A is opted in; do we walk B's events?) is delegated to the EventLedgerReader implementation, NOT enforced at the harvester layer. The MT-169 negative-path test for non-transitivity uses two PEER sessions in the same ledger, not a true parent-child spawn relationship.
